name: Patent Search MCP Server
what_it_does: Patent Search MCP server enabling AI agents to search and retrieve patent information — querying Google Patents or USPTO for patent filings, retrieving patent details and claims, searching prior art, and integrating patent databases into agent-driven IP research, R&D, and competitive intelligence workflows.
use_cases: Searching for patents by technology keyword from IP research agents
Retrieving patent claims and abstracts for prior art analysis from R&D agents
Identifying competitor patents in a technology space from competitive intelligence agents
Checking freedom-to-operate for technology from IP strategy agents
Building patent landscaping reports from IP analytics agents
Monitoring new patent filings in a domain from IP monitoring agents
not_for: Filing patents (search only; patent filing requires legal services)
Legal patent opinions (informational tool, not a substitute for patent attorney)
Real-time global patent coverage (may lag USPTO/EPO latest filings)
best_when: An agent needs to search patent databases for prior art, competitive intelligence, or IP landscape analysis — particularly for R&D teams and IP professionals.
avoid_when: You need legal patent opinions, filing capabilities, or real-time patent prosecution status.

auth.notes: May use Google Patents API (requires API key) or public USPTO data. Configuration varies by data source used. Some sources require API key.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Google Patents public access is free; USPTO bulk data is free
pricing.paid_tiers: Commercial patent databases (Derwent, Espacenet Pro) add cost
pricing.notes: Google Patents data is publicly accessible. USPTO data is freely available. Community MCP server is free. Professional patent databases may add cost if used as backend.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on data source; Google Patents may rate-limit automated access

known_agent_gotchas: Patent data structures vary by office (USPTO, EPO, WIPO) — query syntax differs
Google Patents may block automated scraping without official API access
Patent claims are legal text — agents need domain knowledge to interpret correctly
Prior art search quality depends on search strategy — keyword search has limitations
Community MCP from individual contributor — limited documentation
Patent numbers have office-specific formats (US7654321B2 vs EP1234567A1)
agent_readiness.security_notes: Public patent data — no sensitive credentials typically needed. Community MCP. If using Google Patents API key, store as environment variable.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public patent data. Minimal credentials. Community MCP.
